teachingCaption: She who existed before time — the primordial darkness from which all light is born.
altText: Ādyā Kālī (आद्या काली) — Adya Kali, a devotional image of Maa Adya Kali

teachingCaption: The formless one given form — radiant against the void.
altText: Ādyā Kālī (आद्या काली) — Adya Kali, a devotional image of Maa Adya Kali

teachingCaption: The benevolent south-facing form — she who grants liberation with her right hand extended.
altText: Dakṣiṇa Kālī (दक्षिण काली) — Dakshina Kali, a devotional image of Maa Adya Kali

teachingCaption: The great time itself — she who swallows all universes at the end of each cycle.
altText: Mahākālī (महाकाली) — Maha Kali, a devotional image of Maa Adya Kali

teachingCaption: The auspicious one — fierce in protection yet gentle as a mother.
altText: Bhadra Kālī (भद्र काली) — Bhadra Kali, a devotional image of Maa Adya Kali

teachingCaption: The Mother who loves without condition, who destroys only to create anew.
altText: Kālī Mā (काली माँ) — Kali Ma, a devotional image of Maa Adya Kali

teachingCaption: She dances on Shiva — not in victory over him, but as shakti animating pure consciousness.
altText: Kālī Mā (काली माँ) — Kali Ma, a devotional image of Maa Adya Kali

teachingCaption: Dark as the night sky, beautiful as the deep ocean — Shyama, the beloved dark one.
altText: Śyāmā Kālī (श्यामा काली) — Shyama Kali, a devotional image of Maa Adya Kali

teachingCaption: In her darkness, no shadow can hide — she is the light that illuminates from within.
altText: Śyāmā Kālī (श्यामा काली) — Shyama Kali, a devotional image of Maa Adya Kali

teachingCaption: She who protects her devotees with ferocity that knows no equal.
altText: Rakṣā Kālī (रक्षा काली) — Raksha Kali, a devotional image of Maa Adya Kali

teachingCaption: Beyond the ten directions, beyond all time — Mahakali, the supreme power.
altText: Mahākālī (महाकाली) — Maha Kali, a devotional image of Maa Adya Kali

teachingCaption: Four-armed, garlanded with skulls — each skull a letter of the alphabet she has transcended.
altText: Dakṣiṇa Kālī (दक्षिण काली) — Dakshina Kali, a devotional image of Maa Adya Kali

teachingCaption: Her lotus feet — more merciful than a thousand mothers combined.
altText: Bhadra Kālī (भद्र काली) — Bhadra Kali, a devotional image of Maa Adya Kali

teachingCaption: The cremation-ground goddess — she who dissolves all that is impermanent.
altText: Śmaśāna Kālī (श्मशान काली) — Shmashana Kali, a devotional image of Maa Adya Kali

teachingCaption: Star of the night — she who guides seekers across the ocean of existence.
altText: Tārā Kālī (तारा काली) — Tara Kali, a devotional image of Maa Adya Kali

teachingCaption: The first — before creation there was only her breath moving over the void.
altText: Ādyā Kālī (आद्या काली) — Adya Kali, a devotional image of Maa Adya Kali

teachingCaption: She holds the severed head to remind us: the ego must die for the self to live.
altText: Kālī Mā (काली माँ) — Kali Ma, a devotional image of Maa Adya Kali

teachingCaption: The crimson one — passionate, fierce, and overflowing with the life-force of all beings.
altText: Rakta Kālī (रक्त काली) — Rakta Kali, a devotional image of Maa Adya Kali

teachingCaption: The poet Ramprasad called her Mother — and she answered in the language of the heart.
altText: Śyāmā Kālī (श्यामा काली) — Shyama Kali, a devotional image of Maa Adya Kali

teachingCaption: Ten-armed, ten-faced — Mahakali who encompasses all directions of time and space.
altText: Mahākālī (महाकाली) — Maha Kali, a devotional image of Maa Adya Kali

teachingCaption: She who is called by a thousand names — yet remains beyond all naming.
altText: Ādyā Kālī (आद्या काली) — Adya Kali, a devotional image of Maa Adya Kali

teachingCaption: Standing on the heart of Shiva — she is the power without which stillness itself cannot exist.
altText: Dakṣiṇa Kālī (दक्षिण काली) — Dakshina Kali, a devotional image of Maa Adya Kali
